WAGGOMAN IS A NAMED PARTY IN THE SUTTER CASE (CVCS 11-0470). IF MR. WAGGOMAN IS NOT NAMED IN THE SUTTER CASE, THE DEMURRER WILL BE SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AND A NEW COMPLAINT NOT LONGER THAN 20 PAGES MUST BE FILED. MR. WAGGOMAN TO PROVIDE A COPY OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED ON 9/7/11 AT SUTTER COUNTY. THE COURT WILL PREPARE ITS OWN ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TYRONE ADAMS' MOTION REQUESTING COORDINATION OF CASES (SUTTER COUNTY CVCS-11-0470 AND SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY CGC-11-512166). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MR. ADAMS FAILS TO SHOW THAT COORDINATION IS MORE EFFICIENT THAN STAY OF ALL BUT ONE OF THE ACTIONS HE FILED PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF ONE ACTION OR THAT, EVEN IF COORDINATION IS APPROPRIATE, THE COORDINATED CASES SHOULD BE IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY RATHER THAN SUTTER COUNTY WHERE THE PROPERTY IS LOCATED AND WHICH MAY BE CLOSER TO HIS RESIDENCE THAN IS SAN FRANCISCO. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF CONTESTS THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING. AFTER REVIEWING THE PLEADINGS AND LISTENING TO ORAL ARGUMENTS THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION REQUESTING RELIEF FROM JUDGMENT SUSTAINING DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PUT FORWARD NO FACTUAL OR LEGAL BASIS FOR THIS COURT TO CONCLUDE THAT RES JUDICATA DID NOT APPLY TO BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION REQUESTING RELIEF FROM JUDGEMENT DENYING VICTIM RESTITUTION IS DENIED. THE COURT IS UNCLEAR WHICH JUDGMENT PLAINTIFF IS REFERRING TO. Notice Of Motion; Motion Requesting Direct Victim Restitution And Relief From Judgment Sustaining Defendants' Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint And Relief From Judgment Denying Victim Restitution; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Tyrone Adams; Verified Affidavits Tyrone Adams
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Tyrone L. Adams; 407-716-0233; plaintiff in pro per, appearing via CourtCall on their own behalf.
No other appearances.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion Requesting Relief from Judgment Sustaining Defendants' Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff has put forward no factual or legal basis for this Court to conclude that res judicata did not apply to bar Plaintiff's claims.

Plaintiff's Motion Requesting Relief from Judgement Denying Victim Restitution is DENIED. The Court is unclear which judgment Plaintiff is referring to. To the extent Plaintiff is referring to the criminal misdemeanor order entered in Sutter County, Plaintiff has provided no authority allowing this Court to set aside another court's order.

Plaintiff's Motion Requesting Direct Victim Restitution is DENIED. Plaintiff is not entitled to restitution because all claims have either been adjudicated or dismissed.


Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Joinder In Motion To Lift Stay
Off Calendar Oct-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tyrone Adams, Esq. 407-716-0233 Plaintiff in Pro Per through Court Call.
Keith Haskins, Esq. 714-979-7999 of Morrow & White for Defendant Bruce Buttacavoli & Buttacavoli Industries, Inc. through Court Call.
April Perkins, Esq. 916-929-1481 of Porter Scott for Defendant Chales Easley et al. through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. Ms. Perkins responds. Mr. Haskins agress with the Court's tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ATTORNEY JOHN JUDSON WAGGOMAN's Joinder In Motion To Lift Stay.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendants' motion to lift stay and abatement is granted.

While Cal. R. Ct. 3.515 does not apply here, the Court has the power to grant the motion in light of its inherent power to "amend and control its process and orders so as to make them conform to law and justice." (Code Civ. Proc. 128(a)(8).) On June 2, 2016, this court granted a stay in this case pending final resolution of case No. C081016 in the Third District Court of Appeal. The Third District has finally resolved that appeal. On May 23, 2019, the Third District held that the June 25, 2013 judgment Plaintiff was seeking to appeal was final and not appealable and modified the award of attorney fees and costs. (Perkins Decl. ISO Mt. to Lift, Ex. E.) On June 19, 2019, the court denied Plaintiff's petition for rehearing, and the court has since issued its remittitur. (Id., Ex. F.) Because the original reason for a stay no longer exists, the stay should be lifted.

That Defendant Buttacavoli Industries is protected by an automatic stay pursuant to Bankr. Code 362 is of no consequence. "In the absence of special circumstances, stays pursuant to section 362(a) are limited to debtors and do not include non-bankrupt co-defendants." (Ingersoll-Rand Financial v. Miller Min. (9th Cir. 1987) 817 F.2d 1424, 1427.) One situation in which courts have found special circumstances is when the party protected by the bankruptcy stay is "an indispensable party" such that "the litigation cannot proceed in his absence and therefore must be stayed as against the third party." (In re James Wilson Assoc. (7th Cir. 1992) 965 F.2d 160, 170.) Plaintiff has not shown that litigation in this matter cannot proceed without Defendant Buttacavoli Industries.

Counsel for Defendants is required to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Requesting Coordination Of Cases
Off Calendar Dec-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF TYRONE ADAMS' MOTION REQUESTING COORDINATION OF CASES (Sutter County CVCS-11-0470 and San Francisco County CGC-11-512166).
Appearances: Tyrone Adams (407.716.0233) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona; Matthew R. Day of Porter Scott (916.929.1481 x344) appearing via Court Call for defendants and responding parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Mr. Adams fails to show that coordination is more efficient than stay of all but one of the actions he filed pending the conclusion of one action or that, even if coordination is appropriate, the coordinated cases should be in San Francisco county rather than Sutter County where the property is located and which may be closer to his residence than is San Francisco. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - TYRONE ADAMS, TEL. NO. (407)916-0233, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. MATTHEW R. DAY OF PORTER SCOTT, TEL. NO. (916) 929-1481 EXT. 344 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS CHARLEEN GOODRICH, JACK BASS, ANN FORD, GEORGE TREFCER, HOMEPOINTE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, ROBERT MACHADO AND EILLEEN STEARMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained as to all causes of action that are the subject of the demurrer. As to the demurring defendants who are named as defendants in the Sutter County action and have not been dismissed from that action, this case is abated until the conclusion of the Sutter County action or such earlier time when any of the defendants are dismissed from that action. As to the demurring defendants who are not named in the Sutter County action or have been dismissed from that action, plaintiff is granted leave to amend his claims but must do so in a complaint that is no longer than twenty pages and is not repetitious or filled with legalistic language. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK
